events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: PVC flue slope back-pitched (condensing) on a furnace venting system. This is a defect - condensing furnace vent pipes must slope toward the furnace (or toward a drain) so condensate flows back to the condensate drain, not away. A back-pitched PVC flue would trap condensate, potentially blocking the vent, causing flue gases (including carbon monoxide) to spill into the space. This is a serious safety hazard.

Key points:
- Back-pitched condensing flue = non-compliant with IRC M1801 and NFPA 54
- Condensate pooling can block vent, causing CO spillage
- Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
- Decision: fail
- Risk: high or critical (CO hazard)
- Work stage: in-progress
- Actions: stop work, re-pitch the flue to proper slope (typically 1/4" per foot toward furnace/drain), verify condensate drainage, test after correction

Let me write the JSON.
